Joanna expressed interest in those flavors and shared an image: a photo of a piece of cake with berries on a plate.

【Nate - Dairy-free Show】On September 14, 2022 at 01:43 PM, Nate stated that last Monday he taught people vegan ice cream recipes on his own cooking show; it was nerve-wracking to put himself out there, but it was a blast, and he picked up a few new recipes. Joanna congratulated Nate on the cooking show and said she would tune in, asking what his favorite dish from the show was. Nate identified coconut milk ice cream as his top dish, describing it as smooth and creamy with a tropical coconut twist, and noted it is dairy-free for lactose-intolerant or vegan options; he shared an image of the ice cream: a photograph of a bowl of ice cream with a spoon on a table. Joanna said it looks amazing and asked him to share the recipe. Nate replied that he would love to share it and that they should spread the joy of dairy-free options, and he asked Joanna to let him know when she makes it. Nate added that coconut milk ice cream is one of his favorites, but he also loves a dairy-free chocolate mousse described as super creamy and tastes like the real thing, and he asked what Joanna's favorite dairy-free sweet treat has been so far. He complimented Joanna on a chocolate cake with raspberries image, said he needs to try baking it, asked what other treats she likes making, and how she made the cake. He expressed interest in trying that dessert and thanked Joanna. 【Joanna - Dairy-free Desserts】On September 14, 2022, 01:43 PM, Joanna stated that dairy-free is a must for desserts, noted that last Friday she made a deeeelish dessert with almond milk and it was good, and asked Nate for favorite dairy-free desserts. Joanna identified her favorite dairy-free treat as a chocolate raspberry tart with an almond flour crust, chocolate ganache, and fresh raspberries; Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a chocolate tart with raspberries on top. Joanna also loves making a dairy-free chocolate cake with raspberries, described as moist and delicious with perfect sweetness; Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a piece of chocolate cake with raspberries on a plate. She makes the cake with almond flour, coconut oil, chocolate and raspberries, and it is her favorite for birthdays and special days. Joanna provided another recipe: a delicious dessert made with blueberries, coconut milk, and a gluten-free crust, so creamy and delicious; Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a piece of cake with walnuts on top. Joanna closed by saying, Glad to help, Nate; let me know if you try it; I’m sure you’ll enjoy it; it was great chatting.

【Ice Cream】Session Date: February 25, 2022. Nate replied, 'I whipped up some chocolate and vanilla swirl.' 【Dairy-free Ice Cream】Dairy-free Ice Cream. Session Date: February 25, 2022. Joanna agreed to receive the recipe, saying she is keen to try it and is always up for something sweet. Nate said he would send the recipe, noting he is all about these desserts and asking for feedback. Joanna said she would keep him posted and loves his creations. On April 17, 2022, at 06:44 PM, Joanna expresses love for the ice cream and wishes to make it the way Nate does. Nate replies that it is dairy-free and easy and offers the recipe. Joanna, lactose intolerant, requests the dairy-free recipe. Nate provides the recipe: coconut milk, vanilla extract, sugar, and a pinch of salt; after chilling in the fridge, the mixture is placed in the ice cream maker and frozen until scoopable. Joanna says the result sounds delicious and plans to try it tonight, thanking Nate for sharing. Nate says he is glad to help and asks to be informed of the outcome. Joanna confirms she will let Nate know how it turns out and thanks for sharing the recipe.

【Vegan Ice Cream Recipe】On June 24, 2022 at 10:55 AM, Nate said he made vegan ice cream last Friday and shared it with some people in his vegan diet group; it was awesome. memories.memory: 【Woodhaven】On July 10, 2022, at 02:34 PM, Joanna congratulated Nate on his success, stated that his hard work had paid off and encouraged him to keep it up, and noted that she had taken a road trip for research for her next movie as a much-needed break to explore new places and gain inspiration. Nate responded that her support meant a lot, expressed interest in her road trip, and asked where she went and whether she discovered any interesting places. Joanna then said she went to Woodhaven, a small town in the Midwest, where she enjoyed lovely scenery and historic buildings and visited the library, which had a cool old book collection, with an image showing a bookshelf filled with many books. Nate asked if she found any cool books there. Joanna replied that she stumbled upon a book from the 1900s with stories and sketches about the town and its people, with an image showing the book and a writing on it and a pen. Nate asked what stood out about it. Joanna stated that Woodhaven has had an interesting past with lots of cool people, and seeing how much it changed sparked ideas for her next script. Nate concluded that real-life stories are the best for inspiration, said he cannot wait to hear about her next one, and encouraged her to keep it up. 【Joanna】On July 10, 2022, at 02:34 PM, Joanna and Nate discussed a new screenplay: Joanna said the screenplay is different from her previous work but has the potential to be awesome and she will keep Nate posted; Nate responded that it should do as well as her last one and encouraged her to keep trying and believe in herself; Joanna said Nate's encouragement means a lot and that he is the best for supporting her writing journey; Nate said he is always here for her and that she has a lot of talent and should keep going. Joanna stated she started on a book recently since her movie did well, and there is an image: Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a person holding a notebook with a handwritten page; that page contains dialogues exploring loss, redemption, and forgiveness; the story is deep and emotional and she is really excited about it. Nate said it sounds awesome and he is excited to see how it all plays out. Joanna said she has never published a book before but this might be the first. Nate said good luck on that and that people will recognise you as the same author of the movie you got published and love the book even more. Joanna said thanks; belief in me means a lot; I'll keep doing my best; thanks for the support. Nate said no problem; he's here for you; your hard work will pay off; believe in yourself and your talent – you're incredible. Image shared by Nate: a photo of a person holding a small turtle in a container.

【Joanna】January 23, 2022, 02:01 PM: Joanna finished her first full screenplay and printed it last Friday; she had been working on it for a while, and it was a relief to have it done. Image shared by Joanna: a photography of a book with a page of text on it. Nate expressed excitement and asked what it's about. Joanna stated that the screenplay is a mix of drama and romance. 【Nate】On January 23, 2022, at 02:01 PM, Nate asked Joanna how she felt now that it's finished and whether she had any new plans for it. Joanna replied that she was experiencing a rollercoaster of emotions—relief, excitement, and some anxiety—over finishing this project, and that she planned to submit it to some film festivals and, hopefully, have producers and directors check it out. On the same day at 02:01 PM, Nate congratulated Joanna and expressed hope that they love it. Joanna replied that it was a mix of emotions and that she hoped for positive feedback and new opportunities.

【Spider-Man Screenplay Debut】Session Date: June 05, 2022. On June 05, 2022 at 02:12 PM, Joanna wrote that yesterday she wrote a few bits for a screenplay that appeared on the big screen yesterday; it was nerve-wracking but inspiring to see her words come alive; an image shared by Joanna showed a Spider-Man poster hanging on a wall. At 02:12 PM, Nate replied with congratulations, saying seeing your hard work pay off must've felt amazing, that it was scary too but awesome, and that you’re so inspiring; by the way, last time we saw each other, I noticed a Spider-Man pin on your purse; is Spider-Man your favorite superhero, or do you have another fave? At 02:12 PM, Joanna replied: Thanks, Nate; it was a real roller coaster, but seeing the hard work pay off was amazing; Spider-Man has always been a favorite of mine—I mean, who doesn't love Peter Parker's struggles between being a hero and being a person? But I'm kind of a sucker for any superhero—everyone has their own rad story and powers; do you have a favorite superhero?

Joanna stated she started on a book recently since her movie did well, and there is an image: Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a person holding a notebook with a handwritten page; that page contains dialogues exploring loss, redemption, and forgiveness; the story is deep and emotional and she is really excited about it. 【Joanna's Writing Advice】On March 24, 2022, at 01:43 PM, Nate asked Joanna for writing advice, remarking, "Wow, that's a lot of books." Joanna answered, "Definitely! At 03:56 PM, Nate remarked on Joanna's passion for writing, noted the turtle theme, and said the turtle's resilience is inspiring, urging her to take courage and keep pushing with her writing; he attached the same image again: Image shared by Nate: a photo of a turtle laying on a bed of rocks and gravel. 【Woodhaven】On July 10, 2022, at 02:34 PM, Joanna congratulated Nate on his success, stated that his hard work had paid off and encouraged him to keep it up, and noted that she had taken a road trip for research for her next movie as a much-needed break to explore new places and gain inspiration. Nate responded that her support meant a lot, expressed interest in her road trip, and asked where she went and whether she discovered any interesting places. Joanna then said she went to Woodhaven, a small town in the Midwest, where she enjoyed lovely scenery and historic buildings and visited the library, which had a cool old book collection, with an image showing a bookshelf filled with many books. Nate asked if she found any cool books there. Joanna replied that she stumbled upon a book from the 1900s with stories and sketches about the town and its people, with an image showing the book and a writing on it and a pen. Nate asked what stood out about it. Joanna stated that Woodhaven has had an interesting past with lots of cool people, and seeing how much it changed sparked ideas for her next script. Nate concluded that real-life stories are the best for inspiration, said he cannot wait to hear about her next one, and encouraged her to keep it up. 【Joanna】On July 10, 2022, at 02:34 PM, Joanna and Nate discussed a new screenplay: Joanna said the screenplay is different from her previous work but has the potential to be awesome and she will keep Nate posted; Nate responded that it should do as well as her last one and encouraged her to keep trying and believe in herself; Joanna said Nate's encouragement means a lot and that he is the best for supporting her writing journey; Nate said he is always here for her and that she has a lot of talent and should keep going. Joanna stated she started on a book recently since her movie did well, and there is an image: Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a person holding a notebook with a handwritten page; that page contains dialogues exploring loss, redemption, and forgiveness; the story is deep and emotional and she is really excited about it. Nate said it sounds awesome and he is excited to see how it all plays out. Joanna said she has never published a book before but this might be the first. Nate said good luck on that and that people will recognise you as the same author of the movie you got published and love the book even more. Joanna said thanks; belief in me means a lot; I'll keep doing my best; thanks for the support. Nate said no problem; he's here for you; your hard work will pay off; believe in yourself and your talent – you're incredible. Image shared by Nate: a photo of a person holding a small turtle in a container.

【Nate and Joanna】April 17, 2022, 06:44 PM: Nate noted they hadn't talked in a while and asked how it was going. Joanna stated she had been reading a lot in the past week and that there are many good books she forgot she owned. Nate added that he probably has loads of books he hasn't read in years, calling it a blast from the past.

【Joanna's Writing】Session Date: May 20, 2022. At 07:49 PM, Nate complimented Joanna's work and asked if it is her third piece. Joanna confirmed it is her third piece, stating she chose to write about this because it's really personal; it's about loss, identity, and connection; it's a story she's had for ages but just got the guts to write, and it was hard, but she's proud of it. Nate said that sounds impressive and noted that she really does like writing about sadness and loss. Joanna affirmed this, explaining she had to be vulnerable and dig deep into those topics; she believes meaningful stories come from personal experiences and feelings; it was scary, but she found that she writes best when she's being true to herself—even if it's hard. Nate concluded that it takes guts to explore experiences and feelings, expressed pride in Joanna for staying strong and being true to herself, and encouraged her to keep it up. 【Joanna's Friends】Session Date: May 20, 2022. At 07:49 PM, Joanna stated that writing and creative projects are what get her through tough times and that she is grateful for her supportive friends. Nate replied that both activities can be therapeutic and that positive relationships can have a great impact. Joanna added that it is nice to have friends who understand and appreciate her work; it is priceless to discuss it together and receive feedback; she shared a look at what she has been working on, describing it as a journey and saying she has made it, along with an image described as a photo of a notepad with a dog on it and a pen. Joanna thanked Nate, saying his support means a lot and that having people cheering her on makes the journey easier. Nate concluded that she should keep going and reach for her dreams, noting that she has tons of talent and potential.

memories.memory: 【Joanna's Movie Premiere】On October 25, 2022, at 08:16 PM, Nate asked Joanna what's been up since they last chatted. Joanna replied that another movie script she contributed to was shown on the big screen last Sunday for the first time; it was such a surreal experience, she felt a mix of emotions but overall a satisfying moment, and she had been waiting for this for a long time. Joanna said it's great, appreciates the small joys like that cute stuffed animal, and it's a nice reminder. Nate agreed that those little things make life better. Nate stated it's about finding those little things and cherishing them, otherwise it's easy to get down.

Nate asked: That must have been amazing. What was your favorite part of it? Joanna answered: Seeing my characters I worked so hard on come alive was my favorite part. memories.memory: 【Joanna's Movie Premiere】On October 25, 2022, at 08:16 PM, Nate asked Joanna what's been up since they last chatted. Joanna replied that another movie script she contributed to was shown on the big screen last Sunday for the first time; it was such a surreal experience, she felt a mix of emotions but overall a satisfying moment, and she had been waiting for this for a long time. Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a box of cards with a quote on it. Nate congratulated Joanna and asked how it was to finally see it on the big screen, sharing a photo holding a videogame controller; Image shared by Nate: a photo of a box with a controller inside of it. Joanna said: It was an amazing experience! I'll never forget seeing all of the characters and dialogue I wrote being acted out - it was such a cool feeling. Having all the hard work and determination I put into writing pay off was definitely rewarding. I know this is the third time it's happened, but its just so awesome! Nate asked: That must have been amazing. What was your favorite part of it? Joanna answered: Seeing my characters I worked so hard on come alive was my favorite part. It felt like they jumped off the page and became real - it was totally surreal. Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a drawing book with a bunch of drawings on it. 【Joanna's Drawings】October 25, 2022, 08:16 PM: Nate complimented Joanna on her drawings and asked what inspired her to create them. Joanna replied that they are visuals of the characters to help bring them alive in her head so she can write better. 【Joanna's Idea Origins】Joanna's Idea Origins. Session Date: October 25, 2022. At 08:16 PM, Nate asked, "That's a cool way to gain insight into your characters. Where did you get your ideas for them?" At 08:16 PM, Joanna replied, "I got ideas from everywhere: people I know, stuff I saw, even what I imagined. It's cool to see how an idea takes shape into a person with their own wants, worries, and wishes." 【Joanna's Movie-Watching Plan】Session Date: October 25, 2022. On October 25, 2022 at 08:16 PM, Nate asked Joanna about the character on the left in the photo. Joanna replied, "You'll just have to watch the movie and find out for yourself." Nate stated he was already planning to watch it, and that discussing it made him want to watch it even more. 【Joanna & Nate: Personal Catch-Up】Session: Joanna & Nate — Personal Catch-Up; Session Date: October 25, 2022. Joanna asked what inspired Nate to start making gaming videos. Nate replied that he is a big fan of gaming and thought it would be a fun idea to start making them himself, hoping to share his love of gaming and connect with others who enjoy it too. Joanna commented that Nate will do great, noting that making videos and connecting with people about gaming is awesome. Nate concluded that it's new to him but he's excited to get started. 【YouTube Audience Feedback】Session Date: November 09, 2022. Nate replied that he is a big fan of gaming videos and thought it would be fun to start making them himself, hoping to share his love of gaming and connect with others who enjoy it too. 【Joanna's guidance】On November 09, 2022 at 05:54 PM, Joanna acknowledged Nate's activity in making videos and connecting with people about gaming. An image shared by Joanna shows a man in a striped suit performing on stage. At 07:44 PM, Nate replied that it sounds like you had an interesting time on stage and that it's always a learning experience, and asked whether you have considered going back to acting and whether that is you in the photo. 【Joanna's Creative Path】Session Date: April 21, 2022. At 07:44 PM, Joanna states that acting was her first passion, but she now shines in writing, which expresses her in a new way, and she may return to acting someday—never say never. At 07:44 PM, Nate says: Go for it; follow your passion for writing, but if acting truly makes you happy, give it a shot as well; who knows what'll happen. Nate said he plans to join a new gaming team after the next tourney, has had a few offers but hasn’t decided yet; it’s a big step and he’s ready for a shake up. Joanna said making a switch could open up new opportunities and wished him luck with picking the right team. Nate concluded that it’s a big decision, but he’s excited for the future and asked how Joanna is doing and if anything exciting is happening on her end.

【Valorant Career】会话日期：2022年11月7日，时间：20:10。Nate 表示在2022年11月5日的 Valorant 大型锦标赛决赛中获胜，看到自己的名字成为冠军是最棒的感觉，比赛确实会激发强烈情绪。他还表示最近在享受比赛并为其他锦标赛做准备，因此非常忙碌，但他很喜欢这份忙碌。Joanna 回复：听起来你现在真的很热爱生活！找到适合自己的事物很棒，忙碌有时会让人疲惫，但最终很有回报，继续保持。Joanna 发送了一张图片：一本带黑边、白色书名的书。Nate 表示每天都很感激能有一份自己喜欢的工作。顺便问，你最近有什么新鲜事吗？其实挺棒的！这些小家伙确实给我的生活带来快乐！看着它们很平静也很迷人。我真的很喜欢它们。你呢，Joanna？什么让你开心？
[November 07, 2022, 08:10 PM] Nate: Hey Joanna! Hope 【Nate's Gaming Setup】Session Date: November 07, 2022. On November 07, 2022 at 08:10 PM, Nate upgraded some of his equipment at home and shared an image of a desk with a computer monitor and a keyboard. Joanna asked whether he uses that computer for gaming. Nate replied, yes, this is where he practices and competes, and he sometimes uses it when playing games with friends. Joanna noted that having a dedicated space for practice and competition should help him stay focused. Nate stated that he loves it and that it's like his own little haven to escape into the virtual world, and shared an image of a desk with two monitors and a laptop. Joanna said it sounds great to have his own gaming setup at home; it must be awesome. Nate concluded that it really is and that it's also really important for his career, otherwise he would never be able to beat his competition, and shared an image of a pair of headphones and a video game controller. 【Xeonoblade Chronicles & Nintendo】Session Date: November 07, 2022. On November 07, 2022, at 08:10 PM, Joanna asked Nate what his favorite game was. Nate replied that he is currently playing the fantasy RPG Xeonoblade Chronicles and that it’s been a blast; he highly reccomend it if you’ve never played it before. Image shared by Nate: a photo of two nintendo game covers with a picture of a group of people. Joanna asked what made him start playing it and whether Xeonoblade Chronicles is a Japanese game series. Nate confirmed that Xeonoblade Chronicles is a Japanese game series, said he is a big fan of Nintendo games, and explained that he had actually been wanting to play this one for a while because his friends have played it and reccomended it. Image shared by Nate: a photo of a woman in a costume holding a glass. Joanna noted that it’s nice when a friend’s reccomendation fits your taste well. Image shared by Joanna: a photo of a captain america costume on display in a museum. Nate concluded that it’s true, and that he loves when Joanna gives him movie reccomendations, usually liking them more then if he were to just watch some random one.

Joanna replied that she would definitely recommend it, as long as you're willing to explain what it is to your friends.

【Cork Board Keepsakes】Session Date: June 05, 2022. At 02:12 PM, Nate asked Joanna to show him a picture of her cork board. Nate stated that family support is invaluable and that it’s good to have those reminders. Joanna concluded that it means a lot and keeps her going.

【Joanna's update on a project and inquiry about Nate's activities】On 2022-01-21 at 19:31, Joanna contacted Nate, noting it has been a while since they last connected, saying she has been working on a project lately and that it’s been pretty cool, and asking whether he has any interesting projects or hobbies.

【Joanna's Writing】Session Date: May 20, 2022. At 07:49 PM, Nate complimented Joanna's work and asked if it is her third piece. Joanna confirmed it is her third piece, stating she chose to write about this because it's really personal; it's about loss, identity, and connection; it's a story she's had for ages but just got the guts to write, and it was hard, but she's proud of it. Nate said that sounds impressive and noted that she really does like writing about sadness and loss. Joanna affirmed this, explaining she had to be vulnerable and dig deep into those topics; she believes meaningful stories come from personal experiences and feelings; it was scary, but she found that she writes best when she's being true to herself—even if it's hard. Nate concluded that it takes guts to explore experiences and feelings, expressed pride in Joanna for staying strong and being true to herself, and encouraged her to keep it up. 【Joanna's Friends】Session Date: May 20, 2022. At 07:49 PM, Joanna stated that writing and creative projects are what get her through tough times and that she is grateful for her supportive friends. Nate replied that both activities can be therapeutic and that positive relationships can have a great impact. Joanna added that it is nice to have friends who understand and appreciate her work; it is priceless to discuss it together and receive feedback; she shared a look at what she has been working on, describing it as a journey and saying she has made it, along with an image described as a photo of a notepad with a dog on it and a pen. Joanna thanked Nate, saying his support means a lot and that having people cheering her on makes the journey easier. Nate concluded that she should keep going and reach for her dreams, noting that she has tons of talent and potential.
